title: PostgreSQL Integration, Loopback HTTP, Fault Injection, and Reconciliation · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for postgresql integration, loopback http, fault injection, and reconciliation and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for disposable end-to-end ingestion harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Start an isolated compatible PostgreSQL 18 database, apply real migrations, seed original data, isolate tests, and prove teardown.
Use httptest origins and custom transports for validators, redirects, DNS decisions, compression, slow bodies, malformed bytes, disconnects, and publisher budgets.
Inject constraint, serialization, deadlock, lock wait, pool exhaustion, cancellation, connection loss, and commit ambiguity with expected recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
